title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · Spiral Data Review
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a versioned read model and audited transition design after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: View Contracts · changed case · Review View Contracts
steps.instruction:
review case — Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 102. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects.

Which action demonstrates View Contracts without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: View Contracts · changed case · Review View Contracts.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy. For View Contracts · changed case · Review View Contracts, keep View Contracts connected to executable relational evidence.
